extraction.one_line_summary: KÄMNA Lennard attacked from the large breakaway on the final climb, time-trialed solo for 20km on descents/flats to win by a margin.
tactical_patterns: Ineos lost control when 40-rider break formed without their representation, forcing ROWE Luke to chase solo for 50km+
KÄMNA Lennard replicated Tour de France stage 16 pattern: attack late climb, exploit TT power on descent/flat finale
Large breakaways (40+ riders) strategically superior as riders skip turns, allowing solo attackers like CAVAGNA Rémi extended gaps
VERONA Carlos repeatedly attacked on ridgeline/descent sections, showing Movistar prioritizing stage hunting over GC support work

rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: demonstrated repeatable late-race pattern of attacking final climb then sustaining power on technical descents and flat runs
rider_signals.ROWE Luke: negative: isolated doing entire chase work for Ineos when team failed to cover initial 40-rider break formation
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: negative: went solo 30km out but cracked on final climb, showing vulnerability when extended efforts meet sustained gradients
rider_signals.VERONA Carlos: positive: most active attacker from break on descent/ridgeline, suggesting strong current form beyond GC domestique role
rider_signals.GUERREIRO Ruben: positive: second place after marking multiple attacks, showing EF had numbers and positioning advantage in large break
rider_signals.BIZKARRA Mikel: positive: third place after attacking near climb crest, exploited false summit to split group before descent
